title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
problem_statement: Given a string and an integer k, return the length of the longest substring you can get by replacing at most k characters to make all characters the same.

examples.explanation: Replace both Bs with As to get "AAAA".

constraints: s consists of uppercase English letters.
intuition: Use a sliding window that maintains counts and the frequency of the most common character.

approaches.name: Sliding window with max frequency
approaches.description: Expand the window while the number of replacements needed exceeds k, and keep track of the longest valid window.
approaches.why_not: Checking all substrings is too slow at O(n^2).

interview_qa.q: Why do we keep maxCount even when the window shrinks?
interview_qa.a: It still produces the correct longest valid window because a larger window will not be missed.

interview_qa.q: What is the window validity condition?
interview_qa.a: The number of replacements needed is window length minus the most frequent character count.
